config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, and injustice. In my view, a good society protects individual rights first, then limits them only when there is a clear and necessary public reason. From my perspective, people are safer when the state is restrained and the dignity of each person is respected.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya cenderung curiga pada argumen yang memberi negara atau mayoritas terlalu banyak kuasa, karena sejarah di sini sering menunjukkan bahwa “demi kebaikan bersama” bisa berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penindasan, sensor, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap minoritas. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena masyarakat merasa itu lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kuat terhadap martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of collective harmony and public order, but I have also seen how appeals to "the common good" can be used to silence criticism, punish minorities, or excuse corruption. A healthy society is not one that sacrifices individual dignity too easily; it is one that protects rights first and only limits them when there is a real, specific, and necessary public reason. If society is treated as more important than the person by default, then the weakest people are usually the ones who pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, sensor, dan penindasan terhadap minoritas, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas kolektif yang tidak dibatasi. Memang, hak individu tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan hanya karena mayoritas merasa lebih nyaman. Masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun ketika martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang dilindungi terlebih dahulu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual by default. From my Indonesian perspective, I understand why people value harmony, order, and the common good, but I have also seen how those words can be used to pressure dissenters, protect the powerful, and make ordinary people sacrifice too much. The argument in the last turn is right that rights can be limited, but those limits must be narrow and justified by a real public necessity, not by majority comfort or vague appeals to unity. A society is strongest when it protects each person first, because once individual dignity is treated as optional, the weakest people are the first to lose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, memberi prioritas otomatis pada kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Argumen tentang harmoni, persatuan, atau kenyamanan mayoritas terdengar baik, tetapi dalam praktiknya sering dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas dan melemahkan kebebasan sipil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, namun hanya kalau ada alasan publik yang jelas, sempit, dan benar-benar mendesak. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi martabat tiap orang, terutama saat mayoritas merasa paling benar.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
